product_overview: A backend API for managing menu items, gallery images, and site configuration for hero and info sections. The service supports public read endpoints and authenticated administrative CRUD operations over a TypeScript, Next.js, and Supabase stack.
core_goals: Provide reliable CRUD APIs for menu and gallery content
Allow authenticated admins to manage hero and info configuration
Return consistent HTTP status codes and structured error responses
Enforce authentication and authorization on write operations
Validate input data and handle malformed or oversized payloads safely

features.name: Menu API
features.description: CRUD operations for menu items with public listing and authenticated create, update, and delete actions.
user_flows: GET /api/menu without authentication -> Receive 200 with an array of menu items -> POST /api/menu with valid authenticated request body -> Receive 201 with the created menu item including its ID -> PUT /api/menu with valid authenticated update payload -> Receive 200 with the updated menu item -> DELETE /api/menu with valid authenticated identifier -> Receive 200 or 204 confirming deletion

POST /api/menu without a token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/menu with invalid or expired token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> DELETE /api/menu with duplicate unique field values -> Receive 409 conflict error response

GET /api/menu with an empty database -> Receive 200 with an empty array -> POST /api/menu with missing required fields -> Receive 400 or 422 validation error response -> POST /api/menu with oversized payload -> Receive 413 payload too large error response

features.name: Gallery API
features.description: CRUD operations for gallery images with public listing and authenticated create, update, and delete actions.
user_flows: GET /api/gallery without authentication -> Receive 200 with an array of gallery images -> POST /api/gallery with valid authenticated image metadata -> Receive 201 with the created gallery image including its ID -> PUT /api/gallery with valid authenticated update payload -> Receive 200 with the updated gallery image -> DELETE /api/gallery with valid authenticated identifier -> Receive 200 or 204 confirming deletion

POST /api/gallery without a token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/gallery with invalid or expired token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> DELETE /api/gallery with a malformed ID or invalid target -> Receive 404 not found error response

POST /api/gallery with XSS content such as <script>alert(1)</script> -> Receive 200/201 with sanitized content and no script execution risk -> POST /api/gallery with SQL injection strings -> Receive a safe validation or handling response without exposing data or crashing -> POST /api/gallery with missing required fields -> Receive 400 or 422 validation error response

features.name: Config Hero API
features.description: Operations for hero configuration with public read access and authenticated updates for the site hero section.
user_flows: GET /api/config/hero without authentication -> Receive 200 with the current hero configuration -> PUT /api/config/hero with valid authenticated configuration changes -> Receive 200 with the updated hero configuration -> GET /api/config/hero again -> Receive 200 with the latest persisted hero configuration

PUT /api/config/hero without a token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/config/hero with invalid or expired token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/config/hero with missing required fields -> Receive 400 or 422 validation error response

PUT /api/config/hero with past, future, or invalid date values where applicable -> Receive 400 or 422 validation error response -> PUT /api/config/hero with wrong data types -> Receive 400 validation error response

features.name: Config Info API
features.description: Operations for information configuration with public read access and authenticated updates for the site info section.
user_flows: GET /api/config/info without authentication -> Receive 200 with the current info configuration -> PUT /api/config/info with valid authenticated configuration changes -> Receive 200 with the updated info configuration -> GET /api/config/info again -> Receive 200 with the latest persisted info configuration

PUT /api/config/info without a token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/config/info with invalid or expired token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error response -> PUT /api/config/info with missing required fields -> Receive 400 or 422 validation error response

PUT /api/config/info with SQL injection strings -> Receive a safe handling response without crashing or exposing data -> PUT /api/config/info with XSS payloads such as <script>alert(1)</script> -> Receive a sanitized response or validation error -> PUT /api/config/info with oversized payload -> Receive 413 payload too large error response
